profile.name: Matt
profile.fitness_goal: Gain 10 to 15 pounds of muscle with simple, practical meals.
profile.notes: Prefer simple meals, easy cleanup, and mobile-friendly planning.

recipes.name: Fiber One Cereal Bowl
recipes.instructions:
Pour the cereal into a bowl.
Add the milk.
Eat right away before the cereal gets soggy.
recipes.notes: This is a simple low-effort breakfast that helps use what you already have at home.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Berry Bowl
recipes.instructions:
Add the Greek yogurt to a bowl.
Let the frozen berries sit for 3 to 5 minutes so they soften a little.
Spoon the berries over the yogurt.
Sprinkle the granola on top.
Drizzle the honey over everything and eat right away.
recipes.notes: If you want more flavor, add a pinch of cinnamon. If the berries are too icy, microwave them for 10 to 15 seconds first.

recipes.name: Protein Overnight Oats
recipes.instructions:
Add the oats, milk, protein powder, and chia seeds to a jar or container.
Stir well until the protein powder is fully mixed in and there are no dry clumps.
Stir in the berries.
Cover the container and place it in the refrigerator overnight.
In the morning, stir once more and eat it cold.
recipes.notes: If you want it sweeter, mix in a small drizzle of honey. If it looks too thick in the morning, add a splash of milk and stir again.

recipes.name: Banana Protein Smoothie
recipes.instructions:
Break the banana into smaller pieces and place it in the blender.
Add the milk, peanut butter, and protein powder.
Blend for 20 to 30 seconds until smooth.
Stop and scrape down the sides if powder is stuck.
Pour into a glass and drink right away.
recipes.notes: If the smoothie is too thick, add a little more milk. If you want it colder, add a few ice cubes before blending.

recipes.name: Turkey Hummus Wrap
recipes.instructions:
Lay the wrap flat on a plate or cutting board.
Spread the hummus across the middle of the wrap.
Add the turkey slices in an even layer.
Add the spinach on top.
Sprinkle on the seasoning blend, plus a pinch of salt and pepper if you want more flavor.
Roll the wrap tightly, cut it in half, and eat.
recipes.notes: A simple seasoning combo here is a light shake of garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Do not overfill the wrap or it will tear.

recipes.name: Tuna Crackers Lunch Plate
recipes.instructions:
Open the tuna packet and place it on a plate or in a bowl.
Season the tuna with a pinch of salt, pepper, or garlic powder if you want extra flavor.
Put the crackers on the plate.
Add the spinach on the side.
Wash the apple and serve it with the plate.
recipes.notes: If you want the spinach to taste better, add a tiny pinch of salt and pepper or dip a few bites in a little sauce you already have.

recipes.name: Microwave Potato Protein Bowl
recipes.instructions:
Wash the potato and poke it several times with a fork.
Place it on a microwave-safe plate and microwave for 4 to 5 minutes.
Flip it over and microwave 3 to 5 more minutes until it feels soft when squeezed with a towel.
Cut the potato open carefully.
Top it with cottage cheese, spinach, and shredded cheese.
Microwave for 20 to 30 seconds more if you want the cheese to melt.
recipes.notes: Add salt, pepper, and garlic powder on top to make this taste much better. If the potato is still firm in the middle, microwave it 1 minute at a time until soft.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese Fruit Cup
recipes.instructions:
Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl.
Add the berries on top.
Drizzle with honey.
Stir if you want everything mixed together, or eat it layered.
recipes.notes: If the berries are very frozen, let them sit for a few minutes first so the snack is easier to eat.

recipes.name: Protein Shake and Rice Cakes
recipes.instructions:
Add the milk and protein powder to a shaker bottle.
Close the lid tightly.
Shake for 15 to 20 seconds until smooth.
Pour into a glass or drink from the bottle.
Eat the rice cakes on the side.
recipes.notes: If you want more flavor on the rice cakes, spread a little peanut butter on top.

recipes.name: Apple Trail Mix Snack
recipes.instructions:
Wash the apple.
Put one serving of trail mix into a bowl or small container.
Eat the apple with the trail mix on the side.
recipes.notes: This one is intentionally simple. Use it on busy days when you want something instant.

recipes.name: Air Fryer Chicken Rice Broccoli
recipes.instructions:
If the chicken is frozen, move it to the fridge the night before so it can thaw.
Pat the chicken dry with a paper towel.
Rub the chicken with olive oil, then season it with the seasoning blend, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
Preheat the air fryer to 380 F.
Place the chicken in the air fryer basket and cook for 10 minutes.
Flip the chicken and cook 5 to 8 more minutes, until the inside reaches 165 F.
Put the broccoli in a microwave-safe bowl with a splash of water, cover loosely, and microwave 3 to 4 minutes until tender.
Warm the rice in the microwave for 1 to 2 minutes.
Slice the chicken and serve it over the rice with the broccoli.
recipes.notes: If you do not have a thermometer, cut into the thickest part of the chicken and make sure it is fully white inside with no pink. Add more salt and pepper after cooking if it tastes flat.

recipes.name: Crock Pot Salsa Chicken Bowls
recipes.instructions:
If the chicken is frozen, move it to the fridge the night before.
Place the chicken in the Crock Pot.
Season it with 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
Pour the salsa over the chicken.
Cook on low for 6 to 7 hours or on high for 3 to 4 hours, until the chicken shreds easily.
Use two forks to shred the chicken directly in the sauce.
Warm the black beans in the microwave for about 1 minute.
Cook or warm the mixed vegetables until hot.
Warm the rice.
Serve the salsa chicken over rice with black beans and vegetables.
recipes.notes: Taste the chicken after shredding. If it needs more flavor, add another pinch of salt, pepper, or garlic powder. This is a good beginner dinner because the slow cooker does most of the work.

recipes.name: Oven Turkey Meatballs and Sweet Potatoes
recipes.instructions:
Preheat the oven to 425 F.
Wash the sweet potatoes and cut them into small cubes.
Toss the sweet potatoes with olive oil, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
Spread the sweet potatoes on a sheet pan.
Add the turkey meatballs to the same pan, leaving a little space around them.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring the potatoes once halfway through.
While they bake, cook the broccoli until tender using the microwave or stovetop.
Cut the avocado in half, remove the pit, and slice or scoop out the flesh.
Serve the meatballs with the roasted sweet potatoes, broccoli, and avocado.
recipes.notes: If the sweet potatoes still feel firm, keep roasting them in 3 minute bursts until tender. You can finish the plate with a little extra salt and pepper after everything is cooked.

supplements.name: Multivitamin
supplements.notes: Supports micronutrient coverage when diet variety is lower.

supplements.name: Transparent Labs Mass Gainer
supplements.notes: Optional higher-calorie supplement for feedback tracking.
